weather_report:
Night has settled over Subiaco with the streets darkened to a soft sheen; a light rain is due around 11, tapering toward the small hours as the sky breaks into ragged pieces of cloud. It won’t be sunny tonight, of course, and the UV index is effectively asleep; even toward dawn any light will be filtered and gentle, with clarity improving only in fits between the clouds. The mood is calm and faintly reflective, the sort of weather that hushes traffic and sharpens the scent of wet leaves.

It’s a pleasant evening if you’re dressed for the damp: a light waterproof layer, closed shoes with some grip, and a compact umbrella if you’re wandering. If you’re staying in, finish that half-read paperback, set out tomorrow’s essentials, or step outside between showers to feel the cool air on your face. Early risers should expect a mottled sky rather than a blazing sunrise; plan sun protection later in the day if you’ll be out once the sun gathers strength.
